title: botrychium lanceolatum subsp. typicum
intro: Botrychium lanceolatum subsp. typicum, also known as the lance-leaved grape fern, is a type of rare and endangered fern native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of lance-leaved grape fern include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and providing antioxidant protection, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of lance-leaved grape fern can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
